meaning: adj.死后的,死后发生的n.尸体检查,验尸
examples: A postmortem was carried out to establish the cause of death.
A postmortem examination found that she died from asphyxiation.
Viruses are much more difficult to identify in postmortem analysis than bacteria are.
Mr. McMahon said movement in babies' limbs could have been because of postmortem spasms.
Doctors at Bellevue Hospital Center in Manhattan delivered the boy by postmortem caesarean section.
